after.objective: Implement PER-60/P7-T01 route graph data model in discrypt-transport: per group/channel, a local admitted member has route intents and edges for every admitted remote peer. Preserve provider-as-signaling-only, distinguish direct WebRTC, configured TURN-backed WebRTC, and pending/unavailable states, and do not implement runtime maps, fanout, overlay relay, diagnostics export, voice, or packaging.
after.evidence: Multica PER-60 assigned scope and metadata require unit tests for 3, 8, and 16 member route graphs plus serialization compatibility for existing two-person flow.

steering.evidence: Multica PER-60 assigned scope and metadata require unit tests for 3, 8, and 16 member route graphs plus serialization compatibility for existing two-person flow.

goals.title: PER-52 P6-T01 voice state not persisted as joined. Acceptance: app re...
goals.objective: PER-52 P6-T01 voice state not persisted as joined. Acceptance: app restart/load must derive joined state only from an active backend media session; stale persisted voice_session or voice_channel active_context must not restore as joined. Scope: backend/Tauri state persistence and focused restart regression evidence; do not add device selection, media signaling bridge, group fanout, overlay, or UX polish.
goals.evidence: Local implementation and verification passed: added stale_persisted_joined_voice_session_is_cleared_on_restart in apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/lib.rs plus .omx/plans/P6-T01-voice-state-not-persisted-joined-2026-06-20.md. Post-cleanup checks passed: targeted desktop backend restart test, cargo fmt --check, git diff --check. AI slop cleanup found no new masking fallback in the added test. Blocker: this session exposes no native task tool or .codex/agents for independent code-reviewer and architect lanes; tool discovery exposed GitHub review tools only. Per Ultragoal final gate, independent review unavailable blocks marking aggregate goal complete.

goals.title: PER-53 audio device selection
goals.objective: Implement PER-53 P6-T02 audio input/output selector enumeration and persisted preferences with backend/native plus UI smoke evidence. Preserve backend-derived voice session truth and do not broaden into mute/VAD, signaling bridge, loopback smoke, or group voice.
goals.evidence: Multica PER-53 assigned scope: audio device selection; metadata requires enumeration, input/output preference persistence, fail-closed unavailable behavior, Rust/Tauri/media tests, and Playwright or harness smoke.

goals.title: PER-56 provider-signaled voice media bridge
goals.objective: Implement PER-56 P6-T05 two-person media signaling bridge: sealed voice offer/candidate/native media proof signals must traverse a provider-signaled WebRTC text/control runtime, not manual copy. Preserve provider-as-signaling-only and do not expand into mute/VAD, volume, group overlay, or broader release gates.
goals.evidence: QA rejection fixes: normal all-targets/clippy compile passes without transport harness export leakage; production-copy/honesty/G128 gates pass; PER-56 plan verification command corrected; WebDriver script now records provider-runtime pump evidence path and classifies manual command bridge as non-PER56 fallback; WebDriver run remains blocked by missing DISPLAY/WAYLAND_DISPLAY with manifest at target/per56-g012-native-voice/tauri-webdriver-integrated-manifest.json.

goals.title: PER-56 native WebDriver evidence or waiver
goals.objective: Resolve PER-56 final verification blocker after QA re-test: provide a display-capable Tauri WebDriver/two-profile native proof for PR head 8e0a29a36c9320573a76f7837b30337d6b1294e3 showing provider-signaled runtime traversal for voice signaling, or obtain architect-level waiver/reclassification that the green Rust/Tauri harness plus CI evidence is sufficient despite the original WebDriver requirement.
goals.evidence: QA re-test on 2026-06-20 confirms code gates and CI are green, but QA rejected because required native WebDriver proof remains unavailable. Engineer fresh attempt wrote target/per56-g012-native-voice-engineer-retest/tauri-webdriver-integrated-manifest.json with status failed-preflight: no DISPLAY/WAYLAND_DISPLAY, missing WebKitGTK/JSC 4.1 pkg-config packages, missing native WebDriver binary, and no built app binary before preflight.

goals.title: PER-57 mute VAD speaking
goals.objective: Implement PER-57 P6-T06 mute/VAD/speaking: backend self-mute must toggle only an active voice session; speaking indicators must derive from media-level/VAD events or backend remote-media evidence, not UI optimism. Preserve provider-as-signaling-only and do not expand into volume controls, loopback release smoke, group overlay, or broader release gates.
goals.evidence: Implemented PER-57 fail-closed self-mute and backend-evidence-only speaking UI boundaries. Changed apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/lib.rs, apps/ui/src/main.tsx, apps/ui/tests/e2e/stateful-ui.spec.ts, and .omx/plans/P6-T06-mute-vad-speaking-2026-06-20.md. Passed cargo fmt --check, git diff --check, jq goals validation, and targeted discrypt-media mute_control and voice_activity_detector tests; desktop/UI integration commands are blocked in this runtime by disk and missing node_modules.

goals.title: PER-58 output and mic volume
goals.objective: Implement PER-58 P6-T07 output/mic volume: persisted app-wide microphone gain and app output volume must survive reload, drive native/Rust media proof generation and UI voice/sound output paths, and be covered by deterministic media/audio harness tests. Preserve backend-derived voice truth, provider-as-signaling-only, and do not expand into group voice, overlay relay, release smoke, or hardware-level volume claims.
goals.evidence: Implemented PER-58 persisted app-wide mic gain and output volume. Verification passed: cargo fmt --check with CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target/per58; cargo test -p discrypt-media app_audio_gain; desktop preferences_use_app_config_ids_and_persist_across_reload; desktop native_voice_media_uses_persisted_microphone_gain; npm --prefix apps/ui run typecheck after npm ci with workspace cache. Evidence is local media/Tauri/UI contract evidence, not production two-machine proof.

goals.title: PER-59 human or loopback release smoke
goals.objective: Produce PER-59/P6-T08 release-smoke evidence branch and harness contract: native Tauri/WebDriver or display/audio-capable loopback artifact must prove Discrypt voice join, backend self-mute, speaking/VAD media evidence, mic gain/app output volume behavior, authenticated native media loopback, and leave cleanup. Preserve provider-as-signaling-only and do not claim production evidence from browser shims or raw Pulse capture.
goals.evidence: QA rejection f72c8928: no expected branch/PR exists; backend/browser substitute evidence passed, but native Tauri/hardware-loopback evidence is missing and current runner lacks DISPLAY/WebKitWebDriver/native audio devices.
goals.failureReason: Branch multica/P6-T08-human-loopback-release-smoke pushed and draft PR 59 opened. Harness now emits per59_release_smoke matrix for join, mute, speaking/VAD, mic gain/app output volume, remote participant volume, native loopback, leave cleanup, and production eligibility. Local checks passed: PER-59 guard, dry-run manifest, targeted desktop voice regression, git diff --check, package JSON parse. Production-ready PER-59 artifact remains blocked in this runner by no DISPLAY/WAYLAND_DISPLAY and no native WebDriver/audio hardware; QA must run the documented native Tauri/WebDriver command on a display/audio-capable runner.

Preserve provider-as-signaling-only, distinguish direct WebRTC, configured TURN-backed WebRTC, and pending/unavailable states, and do not implement runtime maps, fanout, overlay relay, diagnostics export, voice, or packaging.
goals.evidence: Implemented PER-60 route graph data model in crates/transport/src/route_graph.rs and exported it from crates/transport/src/lib.rs. Verification passed: cargo test -p discrypt-transport route_graph; cargo test -p discrypt-transport --features mqtt-adapter,nostr-adapter,ipfs-pubsub-adapter,discrypt-quic-rendezvous-adapter route_graph; cargo fmt --check; git diff --check. Evidence is local Rust model/unit evidence, not runtime/split-machine production route evidence.

goals.title: PER-61 P7-T02 runtime map
goals.objective: Replace the single Tauri TextControlTransportRuntime slot with a per-peer runtime map, preserve existing two-person text/control flow, keep provider adapters signaling-only, and add focused regression tests.
goals.evidence: Multica PER-61 acceptance criteria and metadata require per-peer runtime map keyed by admitted remote peer/group-channel scope while preserving migration and old two-person flow.

goals.title: PER-62 per-peer direct/TURN attach
goals.objective: Implement PER-62/P7-T03 per-peer direct/TURN attach: for an active admitted group, derive one text/control runtime attachment per admitted remote peer from backend roster/OpenMLS-governed state, start independent direct or configured TURN-backed WebRTC runtimes keyed by remote peer, preserve independent pending/error state, fail closed for missing/unadmitted/revoked peers, and do not add message fanout, diagnostics export, overlay relay, voice expansion, packaging, or release-gate work.
goals.evidence: PER-62 implementation committed as b150d92, pushed to branch multica/P7-T03-per-peer-direct-turn-attach, and opened as draft PR #62. Verification passed: group_text_runtime_attachments, per_peer_group_runtime_attach_prepares_independent_pending_entries, text_control_runtime_map, text_control_runtime_attach_dedupe_is_remote_peer_scoped, attach_text_control_transport_runtime, group_text_runtime_attachment, full desktop lib suite 141 tests, cargo fmt --check, and git diff --check. Evidence is local Rust/Tauri backend unit/harness evidence, not split-machine production route evidence.

goals.title: PER-63 message fanout/dedup
goals.objective: Implement PER-63/P7-T04 message fanout/dedup: group text/control sends one protected envelope over every admitted per-peer direct/TURN WebRTC runtime route, dedups duplicate route deliveries/receipts idempotently, reports pending/missing/revoked/unavailable peer routes explicitly without blocking valid peers, preserves two-person text flow, and keeps providers signaling-only with no application relay fallback.
goals.evidence: PER-63 implementation verified locally: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --manifest-path apps/desktop/src-tauri/Cargo.toml --lib; cargo test --manifest-path apps/desktop/src-tauri/Cargo.toml --lib -- --test-threads=1 (144 passed); git diff --check. Ready for PR/QA; aggregate goal remains open for unrelated pending/review-blocked stories.

goals.title: PER-64 route diagnostics export
goals.objective: Implement PER-64/P7-T05 route diagnostics export: support bundle includes redacted group route graph edges, per-peer runtime edge state, ICE/DTLS/DataChannel/TURN usage, and fanout/dedup route evidence without provider application relay.
goals.evidence: QA rejection fixed: route diagnostics now derives TURN configured/effective inputs from each graph's channel policy when present, otherwise its group policy, instead of active_context. goals.title: PER-65 route cleanup on leave/revoke
goals.objective: Implement PER-65/P7-T06 route cleanup on leave/revoke: close pending and attached per-peer direct/TURN WebRTC routes for removed/offline/revoked peers, reject stale outbound retries and inbound frames from no-longer-admitted route peers, preserve valid admitted peer routes, and keep providers signaling-only with no application relay fallback.
goals.evidence: Second QA rejection fixed: validate_group_route_sender now fails closed when no current remote member matches sender_device_id even if the roster has no signer metadata, while legacy/OpenMLS valid receive paths seed explicit current sender membership evidence. OpenMLS admission Welcome frames now carry owner_member_id and persist owner signer/member evidence on join. Added receiver_command_rejects_unknown_group_sender_without_signer_roster regression proving no receipt/message is recorded and text_envelope_verification_failed is surfaced for an unknown removed sender. Verification passed: g012 two-profile delivery, receiver OpenMLS plaintext, unknown no-signer sender regression, pending-runtime attach, route_cleanup (3), group fanout (1), route diagnostics (2), full desktop backend lib suite 150/150, cargo fmt --check, git diff --check.

goals.title: PER-63 message fanout/dedup

goals.title: PER-65 route cleanup on leave/revoke

goals.objective: Implement PER-63/P7-T04 message fanout/dedup: group text/control sends one protected envelope over every admitted per-peer direct/TURN WebRTC runtime route, dedups duplicate route deliveries/receipts idempotently, reports pending/missing/revoked/unavailable peer routes explicitly without blocking valid peers, preserves two-person text flow, and keeps providers signaling-only with no application relay fallback.

goals.title: PER-63 message fanout/dedup
goals.objective: Implement PER-63/P7-T04 message fanout/dedup: group text/control sends one protected envelope over every admitted per-peer direct/TURN WebRTC runtime route, dedups duplicate route deliveries/receipts idempotently, reports pending/missing/revoked/unavailable peer routes explicitly without blocking valid peers, preserves two-person text flow, and keeps providers signaling-only with no application relay fallback.
